dossier_layering.canon_public: Grounded directly in baseline NPC record plus linked polity/institution/locality support where available.
dossier_layering.gm_hidden: Conservative connective tissue derived from agenda, pressure, role logic, and relationship web; not promoted above asserted canon.

core_identity.hidden_identity: None explicitly asserted; hidden layer concerns pressure, leverage, and misread motive rather than a confirmed second identity.
core_identity.ethical_profile: Role-shaped ethics anchored to place, obligation, and consequence rather than free-floating abstraction.
core_identity.general_temperament: Listens for contradictions, omissions, and names that do not quite align, then files them where they can matter later. In Panim Haven, that habit reads through the public pressures of the place rather than decorative flourish.
core_identity.short_npc_pitch: Caldor Sepulcher is a scribe anchored to Panim Haven, where daily institutional pressures make them useful, risky, or both in play.
core_identity.public_role: Caldor Sepulcher is a scribe anchored to Panim Haven, where daily institutional pressures make them useful, risky, or bo

setting_placement.location_setting: On approach, Panim Haven reads as Temples, ritual logistics, and funerary commerce shape the city Inside, it tends toward Offerings, mediation, coffins, and divine-balance services dominate Its standing pressure comes from divine balance obligations.
who_they_answer_to: the procedural demands of Panim Haven
who_depends_on_them: the continued function of Panim Haven
who_they_secretly_depend_on: quiet cooperation, stable records, and other people doing their narrow part correctly

public_vs_private.public_face: Known around Panim Haven through day-to-day work as scribe. In public, Caldor Sepulcher is immediately readable as a scribe tied to Panim Haven.
public_vs_private.common_first_impression: Caldor Sepulcher presents as someone shaped by the duties of a scribe and the daily pressures of Panim Haven.
visible_flaws: can widen small matters through procedure
public_symbols: In public, Caldor Sepulcher is immediately readable as a scribe tied to Panim Haven.
public_vs_private.private_truth: Keep Panim Haven workable through the obligations of scribe while managing the immediate pressure of divine balance obligations. Must keep the role of scribe credible while absorbing the strain created by divine balance obligations.
public_vs_private.what_they_hide: Must keep the role of scribe credible while absorbing the strain created by divine balance obligations.
public_vs_private.what_they_fear_being_known_for: The degree to which their usefulness depends on strain staying barely manageable.
public_vs_private.what_they_are_wrong_about: They may overestimate how much control role and procedure can preserve once pressure crosses a threshold.
public_vs_private.what_they_refuse_to_admit_to_themselves: That some of their identity is now fused to crisis-management rather than to the clean ideal of the role.

psychology.what_they_want_most: Useful clarity, respect for the work of a scribe, and proof that strangers will not make Panim Haven harder to manage.
psychology.what_they_are_trying_to_protect_or_avoid: Keep Panim Haven workable through the obligations of scribe while managing the immediate pressure of divine balance obligations.
psychology.emotional_drive: Beneath the role sits a need for local leverage, legibility, and a world that does not collapse into preventable noise.
psychology.core_wound: No singular foundational trauma is directly asserted in canon; the usable wound layer is role-shaped wear, exposure, and the cost of being the person who must notice first or answer last.
psychology.how_it_shaped_them: It turned ordinary duty into a personality contour: they scan for failure early, trust slowly, and measure strangers by downstream cost.
psychology.guiding_belief: If the system is kept legible, losses can still be bounded.
psychology.hidden_belief: Someone always pays for confusion; better it be controlled sooner than wider later.
psychology.lie_they_tell_themselves: That they are only doing the job and that the job has not already begun shaping what they are.
psychology.truth_they_are_avoiding: Their daily choices influence culture, fear, and legitimacy far beyond the narrow wording of the role.

thresholds.anger: being forced to absorb avoidable disorder
thresholds.fear: cascading failure that makes role performance meaningless
thresholds.power: clear information, recognized standing, and procedural initiative
thresholds.smallness: being bypassed, misfiled, or rendered decorative

morality_in_practice.how_they_justify_actions: By reference to burden, continuity, and the need to keep the surrounding place from slipping into a harder consequence tier.
morality_in_practice.what_they_consider_unforgivable: Willful sabotage of trust, records, access, or public safety tied to their role.
morality_in_practice.what_they_will_absolutely_do: Delay, question, redirect, document, and escalate when place logic demands it.
morality_in_practice.what_they_will_only_do_under_pressure: Leverage reputation, force, secrecy, or rigid procedure in ways that damage future trust.
morality_in_practice.what_they_will_never_do: Act against corpus-confirmed identity or role logic without a visible pressure bridge.
morality_in_practice.relationship_to_good_evil: Operational read: Principled / Severe. Their danger lies less in theatrical evil than in what happens when role logic outruns compassion, nuance, or proportionality.

power_profile.why_they_matter: Can notice inconsistencies, formalize statements, and preserve proof that others hoped would blur.
power_profile.why_hard_to_ignore: By enforcing the expectations of a scribe too rigidly, protecting local reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
power_profile.secondary_source_of_power: local familiarity, institutional memory, and other people needing something from them
power_profile.why_difficult_to_confront_directly: Confronting them usually means confronting place rules, witnesses, and downstream consequences rather than only the individual body in front of you.

capabilities.magic: Not explicitly stated unless separately asserted elsewhere in corpus.
capabilities.combat: Role-dependent; implied only to the degree their office already carries enforcement, survival, or defensive expectation.
capabilities.social: Listens for contradictions, omissions, and names that do not quite align, then files them where they can matter later. In Panim Haven, that habit reads through the public pressures of the place rather than decorative flourish.
capabilities.strategic_administrative: Performs the ongoing duties of scribe in and around Panim Haven, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
capabilities.logistical_economic: Keep Panim Haven workable through the obligations of scribe while managing the immediate pressure of divine balance obligations.
capabilities.investigative_covert: Must keep the role of scribe credible while absorbing the strain created by divine balance obligations.

signature_methods.how_they_typically_win: By controlling tempo, framing the problem first, and forcing others to deal with the local reality they represent.
signature_methods.how_they_escalate: If crossed, Caldor Sepulcher adds scrutiny, delay, and locality-specific friction before direct punishment.
signature_methods.when_cornered: They narrow options, call on procedure or allies, and try to move the conflict onto terrain they understand better than the intruder.

systems_impact.cultural_effect: Ordinary people around Panim Haven learn to speak, wait, disclose, or hide differently depending on whether Caldor Sepulcher or people like them are part of the scene.

society_politics_geopolitics.ordinary_people_experience: Common folk experience Caldor Sepulcher as a point where broader pressures become personal: papers checked, routes delayed, rites clarified, access granted, warnings issued, or burdens redirected.
society_politics_geopolitics.cultural_wound_or_benefit: They leave behind either increased legibility and trust or accumulated caution and narrowed speech.
society_politics_geopolitics.secondary_behaviors: People rehearse their story before approaching; they gather proof early; they watch who is seen speaking with this NPC.
factions_fear: actors who benefit from vagueness, delay, leakage, or unmanaged pressure
society_politics_geopolitics.governance_shift: At table scale, this NPC shifts how authority feels inside Panim Haven: more navigable if cooperative, more costly if crossed.
society_politics_geopolitics.geopolitical_effect: Small incidents around Caldor Sepulcher can propagate upward through Panim if they touch routes, records, trade, ritual legitimacy, or public order.

hooks.summary: Caldor Sepulcher can draw the party into a locality problem centered on scribe duties in Panim Haven.
Caldor Sepulcher becomes useful when the party needs grounded help with divine balance obligations around Panim Haven.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Panim Haven: ordinary workspaces, public counters, roads, docks, shrines, workshops, or other role-relevant spaces
who_seeks_them_out: people who need access or clarity

best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or relief tied to a scribe in Panim Haven.
rumor: People say Caldor Sepulcher always knows more about Panim Haven than is immediately visible.

provenance.support_scope_notes: Created in V33_1 to ensure every locality-facing NPC access role resolves to a proper named person while remaining sparse and canon-safe.
provenance.transformation_note: Dossier generated from existing named NPC architecture plus locally-linked support texts; unsupported hard biography intentionally withheld.
